title: Constants in Rust
content:
### Constants in Rust

Constants in Rust are values that are bound to a name and never change. They are declared with `const` or `static`.

---

### `const` vs `static`

| Feature | `const` | `static` |
|---------|---------|----------|
| Inlined at compile time | Yes | No (fixed memory location) |
| Type annotation required | Always | Always |
| Mutable | No (use `static mut` unsafely) | `static mut` is unsafe |
| Global scope | Yes | Yes |

---

### Usage

```rust
const MAX_POINTS: u32 = 100_000;
const LANGUAGE: &str = "Rust";

static APP_NAME: &str = "MyApp";
static mut COUNTER: u32 = 0;  // requires unsafe to mutate
```

Constants can be declared in any scope, including global scope. They must be explicitly typed and can only be set to a constant expression (not a runtime-computed value).

> **Key Point:** Use `const` for compile-time constants that never change. Avoid `static mut` unless absolutely necessary, as accessing it requires `unsafe` blocks and can lead to data races.

quiz.question: Which keyword is used to declare compile-time constants in Rust?
